text:
Mongotune's Observability Stack
1. Separate FTDC Collection (mongotune's own diagnostic data)
Mongotune writes its own FTDC files - completely independent from mongod's FTDC.
Location: <log_dir>/diagnostic.data/mongotune-metrics.*
Config:
general:
ftdc:
directory_path: /var/log/mongotune/diagnostic.data # optional
collection_period_millis: 990
# ~1 second samples
samples_per_archive_chunk: 300
# ~5 min per chunk
archive_file_size_mb: 10
# rotate at 10MB
directory_size_mb: 200
# max 200MB total
What it captures:
- All policy metrics (CPU pressure, dwell times, memory signals, etc.)
- Mongotune's own process metrics (memory, CPU usage)
- Tokio runtime metrics (task queue depth, alive tasks)
- MongoDB driver metrics (commands sent, failures, connection count)
- Timestamps of when mongotune itself was stalled
Key insight from ftdc.rs:136-144:
let elapsed = this_tick.duration_since(prev_tick);
if elapsed >= self.period * 2 {
warn! (logv2.id = 21101, "FTDC collection task was stalled");
}
Mongotune detects when it's being starved by system pressure and logs it.
2. OTLP Export (push to external monitoring)
Mongotune can push metrics to any OpenTelemetry-compatible backend (Prometheus, Datadog, etc.)
Config:
general:
otlp:
endpoint_url: https://your-otel-collector:4318/v1/metrics
push_period_seconds: 60
This enables external monitoring dashboards for mongotune's data.
3. What Mongotune Monitors About Itself
From metrics.rs and driver.rs, mongotune tracks:
Metric
mongotune.core.process.*
mongotune.core.tokio.alive_tasks
mongotune.core.tokio.global_queue_depth
mongotune.core.driver.commands
mongotune.core.driver.failed_commands
mongotune.core.driver.open_connections
Description
CPU, memory, open FDs of mongotune itself
Async tasks running
Task backlog
Commands sent to MongoDB
Failed commands
Connection pool size
4. Policy-Specific Metrics (for rate limiting)
Every signal mongotune monitors is also recorded as a metric:
Metric
Description
cpu_pressure_percent
System CPU load
status_cmd_latency_millis
How long serverstatus took
available_memory_mb
Free memory
oom_score
Kernel OOM score
dwell_time.read.per_ticket_micros
Read queue latency
rate_limit.rate_per_sec
Current rate limit applied
overload_counters.*
How often each signal triggered
